title: Document coverage policy in CLAUDE.md and finalize manifest

title: Document coverage policy in CLAUDE.md and finalize manifest
description: After the metric switch and per-file work lands: (1) Update CLAUDE.md with a 'Coverage notes' section listing inherent gap patterns (WASM-only, Postgres VM-only, binary-entry, macro-derive). (2) Re-baseline the manifest to reflect the new metric and the recovered coverage. (3) Remove this epic when each linked task is closed.

title: Cover server/src/media.rs upload error paths

Add integration tests in server/tests/web_media.rs that POST malformed/oversized multipart bodies.

title: Cover server/src/mailer.rs configuration and send error paths
description: server/src/mailer.rs: from_config invalid-sender, send_via_smtp malformed to-address, mail-capture file-write failure. The lettre Mailbox parse failures for addresses that pass email_address validation but fail Mailbox::from_str are rare; may require synthesizing a Mailbox-illegal-but-EmailAddress-legal string or accepting them as defensive.

title: Cover storage/src/backup/mod.rs helper edges
description: storage/src/backup/mod.rs: ensure_absent existing-path error, mirror_media_directory empty-source path, copy_or_link_media_file parent-creation branch. Specific lines: 360, 452, 454, 496, 503-504, 517, 538, 552, 555, 640, 668, 734, 830. Several straightforward unit tests against a tempdir tree.

title: Cover storage/src/backup/sqlite.rs ROLLBACK paths and validate_foreign_keys failure
description: storage/src/backup/sqlite.rs lines 51-53, 91-96, 140-141, 177: ROLLBACK arms in export/restore + validate_foreign_keys failure path. Provoke by forcing a constraint violation mid-transaction (e.g. a backup with duplicate primary keys, or invalid JSON in a column).

title: Refactor web/src/posts.rs error mappers and parametrize slug attempts
description: Uncovered arms in perform_update_error, create_rendered_post_error (some new tests landed; verify all arms hit), ListByTagError::Internal arms at 566/631-632, the 'unable to allocate unique slug after 100 attempts' branch at 712. Parametrize create_post_with_unique_slug to take max_attempts: usize (default 100 in production); test with max_attempts=2 + seeded slug conflicts to hit the exhaustion arm. Test ListByTagError::Internal via a mock PostStorage returning the error.

title: Cover SlowSpanLayer on_close branches and lock_env poison arm
description: server/src/observability.rs:94, 99, 106, 107: on_close early returns when span lookup fails or SpanStartedAt extension missing — provoke by emitting an event (not a span) while the layer is registered. Line 204: lock_env's Err(poisoned) => poisoned.into_inner() arm — provoke by poisoning the mutex via std::panic::catch_unwind in a held lock.

title: Bind tracing field expressions in web/src/error.rs::server_boundary
description: web/src/error.rs:197-198: tracing::error!(..., public_error = ?error.public(), operator_message = %error.operator_message(), ...). The macro evaluates these expressions inside a record-closure that llvm-cov maps to the macro callsite, not 197/198. Bind to local variables first ('let public = error.public(); let operator = error.operator_message();') and reference them in the macro. Same behavior; correct attribution.

title: Refactor web/src/auth.rs for testable error/auth arms
description: Uncovered: require_auth missing-Parts arm (126-128), current_user non-Unauthorized fallthrough (223), logout unauthenticated branch (351). Refactor by extracting pure helpers (require_auth_with_parts(parts: Option<Parts>), classify_current_user(result: Result<AuthUser, InternalError>), logout_revoke(auth: Option<AuthUser>)). The #[server] fns become one-line wrappers reading use_context::<Parts>() and calling the helper. Test the helpers directly.

title: Extract /api/* handler closure in server/src/lib.rs to named async fn
description: server/src/lib.rs:104-116 — the axum::routing::post(move |req| { ... }) handler body has its lines reported at 0 hits even though every integration test that POSTs /api/* runs it. llvm-cov attributes the closure to the .route() builder callsite, not the body. Extract into 'async fn handle_api_request(state, mailer, secure_cookies, req)' and have the route reference it. Improves both attribution and readability.

title: Replace Poll::Pending test panics in web/src/error.rs server_boundary tests
description: web/src/error.rs:355, 417: tests for server_boundary use manual future.as_mut().poll(&mut cx) with a Poll::Pending => panic!() arm that is structurally unreachable. Rewrite the tests using #[tokio::test] and .await to eliminate the dead arm. Pure refactor; no behavior change.

title: Cover storage/src/render.rs extract_org_title edges and Internal From arm
description: storage/src/render.rs lines 104, 111, 135-139, 149, 154, 164 are extract_org_title edge branches: blank-line-after-KV-block, key-without-value, prefix-without-value, non-#+/heading content after KV. Line 307 is From<UpdatePostError>::Internal. Add ~6 unit tests with carefully crafted org-mode inputs and one From-impl test.

title: Cover sanitize_filename edge cases and Password::verify error arm
description: common/src/media.rs lines 14,18: sanitize_filename early returns for '.', '..', and \0-only inputs. common/src/password.rs line 76: argon2 verify() returning a non-Password Error (e.g. truncated PHC string). Add small unit tests in each file.

title: Switch check-coverage metric from instantiation-weighted lines to source-line
description: summary.lines.percent in cargo-llvm-cov sums across compile units (e.g. when integration tests link the web crate a second time, llvm-cov emits a second set of instantiation counters that no test ever runs). This inflates the denominator and produces spurious manifest 'regressions' on files where every source line is covered. Switch scripts/check-coverage to derive coverage as (total_executable_source_lines - distinct_zero_hit_source_lines) / total_executable_source_lines using cargo llvm-cov report --show-missing-lines as the source. Re-baseline the manifest after the metric change.

title: Restore coverage after storage-crate reorg
description: The storage-crate reorganization caused coverage drops in observability.rs, error.rs, auth.rs, posts.rs, media.rs, render.rs, and a few others as tests that incidentally exercised those paths moved to a different crate. Add targeted tests to restore coverage above baseline.

title: storage crate: extract traits/records from common (Phase 1)

title: Fold author username into PostRecord via JOIN to eliminate N+1
description: list_local_timeline and list_posts_by_tag fetch a page of posts, then loop over each post calling users.get_user(post.user_id) — classic N+1. Fix by joining users into every post-fetching SQL and adding author_username: String to PostRecord, mirroring the tags JSON-aggregation pattern from tags.2.1. Drop the per-row lookups in both endpoints.
close_reason: N+1 eliminated: author_username folded into PostRecord via JOIN; all post-listing queries now single-roundtrip

title: web: define CSS for .error/.success/.j-loading/.draft-banner or rename
description:
Components reference `.error`, `.success`, `.j-loading`, and `.draft-banner` classes (dozens of call sites across web/src/pages/*.rs) but none of these classes are defined in server/assets/jaunder.css or jaunder-themes.css. They render as semantically meaningful but visually identical to surrounding text.

Two options:
1. Define the rules in jaunder.css (preferred — the call sites are already consistent).
2. Rename to the `j-` prefix (`.j-error`, `.j-success`, etc.) and add definitions, matching the convention in docs/web-style-guide.md §7.

Whichever path: ensure error text is visually distinguishable (color contrast), success affirming, and the loading state distinct.

title: Task 4: Refactor web to use common::backup
description: Refactor web/src/backup.rs to use shared backup functions and constants from common::backup.

title: Task 2: Create common/src/backup.rs
description: Create common/src/backup.rs with BackupMode and utilities, export it in lib.rs, and run tests.

title: cargo leptos end-to-end fails 5 tests due to stale test fixtures
description:
cargo leptos end-to-end consistently fails 5 tests that pass in scripts/verify (via nix build .#nix-only-checks):

- tests/auth.spec.ts:49 login with valid credentials
- tests/auth.spec.ts:85 logout page logs out
- tests/auth.spec.ts:101 sidebar reverts to signed-out state
- tests/email.spec.ts:6 email verification flow
- tests/password_reset.spec.ts:6 password reset flow

Root cause: the local end2end/run-e2e.sh does:

    "$BIN" user-create --username testlogin --password testpassword123 2>/dev/null || true

The 2>/dev/null swallows any error. The Nix VM's seed_db() instead:
1. Deletes from users/sessions/invites/email_verifications/password_resets/posts/tags/site_config.
2. Sets registration_policy = open.
3. Creates testlogin AND testnoemail with passwords.
4. Removes mail.jsonl.

The local dev DB (data/jaunder.db) accumulates state across runs (708 stale users observed). testlogin can end up missing or with a non-matching password after, e.g., the password_reset test runs. user-create silently fails (e.g., 'user already exists' or because the running server has locked the row), the script proceeds, and every test that logs in as testlogin fails with 'invalid credentials'.
design: Make end2end/run-e2e.sh mirror Nix seed_db(): wipe stateful tables, set registration_policy, create both fixture users, and remove mail.jsonl. Stop swallowing user-create stderr so a real failure is visible. Cargo-leptos's auto-init creates the schema but doesn't wipe data, so the seeding must do that explicitly.
acceptance_criteria:
- cargo leptos end-to-end finishes with 0 failed.
- Output matches the Nix-VM e2e run (same set of tests pass).
- run-e2e.sh prints user-create errors on stderr rather than hiding them.
close_reason: Fixed via scripts/e2e-local.sh wrapper (per-run temp storage dir) + shared scripts/seed-e2e-fixtures.sh (used by local run-e2e.sh and both Nix e2e checks). cargo leptos end-to-end via the wrapper now passes 41/41 in 9.8s. Also revisited jaunder-3gt: the LocalResource fix was rolled back in favor of defensive use_context in backup.rs (LocalResource awaited in SSR-rendered Suspense hangs the Suspense — see updated memory).

title: Fix tokio-rt-worker panics in backup.rs: SSR Resource fetchers race with owner disposal
description:
Two tokio-rt-worker panics observed in cargo leptos end-to-end:

- web/src/backup.rs:138 (backup_warning_visible) — called via Resource in BackupBanner
- web/src/backup.rs:195 (current_user_is_operator) — called via Resource in Sidebar

Both: 'expected context of type Arc<AppState> to be present'. Same root-cause family as jaunder-nba: the Resource fetcher runs on a tokio worker, and when the per-request reactive owner is disposed (client disconnect, e2e parallelism) the AppState context provided per-owner is gone, so expect_context panics.

These two server fns are uniquely vulnerable because BackupBanner and Sidebar render on every page — eager Resource fetches multiply the race odds. Other server fns using the same expect_context pattern are only invoked via /api/* HTTP, which has its own per-request context provision and no owner-disposal window.
design:
Structural fix (user-chosen): make these Resources client-only by switching from Resource::new to LocalResource::new. Server-side renders without the operator-gated UI; client fills it in after hydration.

User impact:
- Unauthenticated visitors: no change (never saw operator UI).
- Authenticated non-operators: no change.
- Authenticated operators: brief moment after first paint where BackupBanner and operator-conditional sidebar nav items are absent, then pop in after hydration.

Sites to convert:
- web/src/pages/ui.rs:130 (BackupBanner: visible Resource)
- web/src/pages/ui.rs:688-691 (Sidebar: operator Resource)

The companion 'user' Resource in Sidebar uses current_user(), which goes through require_auth() / use_context::<Parts>() (graceful Option path, no panic) — leave it alone.
acceptance_criteria:
- cargo leptos end-to-end shows zero 'tokio-rt-worker.*panicked' lines.
- SSR HTML for unauthenticated and authenticated-non-operator visitors is byte-identical (or trivially equivalent) to pre-fix.
- Authenticated operators still see BackupBanner (when config incomplete) and operator nav items after hydration.
- scripts/verify passes.
close_reason: Fixed in d1353b7. cargo leptos end-to-end: tokio-rt-worker panics 2 -> 0. 36 passed, 5 failed (all pre-existing flakes — auth login/logout, email/password_reset email-capture timing — out of scope per user).

title: Fix tokio-rt-worker panics: Effect::new_isomorphic races with SSR owner disposal
description:
During cargo leptos end-to-end, 22 panics fire from reactive_graph-0.2.13/src/traits.rs:361 with messages like "At web/src/pages/home.rs:65:30, you tried to access a reactive value which was defined at web/src/pages/home.rs:29:17, but it has already been disposed." All on tokio-rt-worker threads (server-side SSR). Root cause: four sites use Effect::new_isomorphic to copy a Resource's resolved value into RwSignals. When the Resource future resolves after the per-request reactive owner is disposed, the effect re-fires server-side and panics on get_untracked/set against the dead signal.

Sites:
- web/src/pages/home.rs:49
- web/src/pages/posts.rs:228
- web/src/pages/posts.rs:249
- web/src/pages/posts.rs:423

Note: only home.rs currently panics in practice; posts.rs sites share the bug latently.
design:
Approach (Option A): stop copying Resource->RwSignal inside an isomorphic effect. Instead:
1. Render initial state directly from the Resource inside <Suspense> so SSR still has data.
2. Keep RwSignals only for state genuinely mutated client-side (appended rows from 'load more', error flashes, cursor state for pagination).
3. Use a client-only Effect::new (no isomorphic) to seed/reset those signals when the Resource refreshes.

This removes the server-side disposal race entirely because:
- Suspense reads the Resource value within the live reactive owner during rendering.
- The mutation signals only need to exist on the client, where there is no async-after-disposal scenario.
acceptance_criteria:
- cargo leptos end-to-end output contains zero 'tokio-rt-worker.*panicked' lines (other test failures out of scope).
- scripts/verify passes.
- SSR still renders timeline rows for both authenticated home feed and unauthenticated local timeline (curl '/' shows post HTML without JS execution).
- 'Load more', refresh-after-publish, and error paths still behave correctly.
notes:
Observed during e2e run on branch 'feeds' on 2026-05-11. Evidence in /tmp/leptos-e2e.log (panic lines: 136, 141, 146, 149, 155, 159, 173, 177, 183, 191, 194, 218, 222, 228, 261, 266, 272, 275, 278, 282, 285, 370). User chose Option A + scope all four sites.

Post-fix follow-up: record two bd memories — (a) anti-pattern: Effect::new_isomorphic copying Resource into RwSignals races with SSR owner disposal; (b) preferred pattern: <Suspense>+resource.get() for initial render, RwSignals for client-side mutation only, client-only Effect::new for seeding. Reference post-fix home.rs and posts.rs as canonical examples.
close_reason: Fixed in 6ba0c03 (home.rs) and 199ec9c (posts.rs). cargo leptos end-to-end: tokio-rt-worker disposal panics 22 -> 0. No regressions traceable to refactor (remaining test failures are pre-existing flakes: DB locks, email-capture timing, auth flow).

title: tags.10: end-to-end exercise
description:
Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-13-tags-ui-design.md Step 10.

Items:
1. E2E: register a user, create a post with three tags via the UI, land on permalink, click a tag chip, verify navigation to /tags/:tag.
2. E2E: from /tags/:tag, navigate back to the post, edit it to remove one tag and add a different one, verify chip set updates in the footer and that the removed tag's listing page no longer contains the post while the added tag's listing does.
3. E2E autocomplete: in the create form, type a partial tag, verify autocomplete dropdown appears with suggestions from existing tags, click one, verify a chip is added.

title: tags.9: tag listing pages + routes
description:
Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-13-tags-ui-design.md Step 9.

Items:
1. Add SiteTagPage at /tags/:tag. Topbar (title='#<tag>', sub='Posts on this instance'), j-scroll + j-page chrome, cursor-paginated PostCard list driven by new list_posts_by_tag server fn wrapping the storage call.
2. Add UserTagPage at /~:username/tags/:tag. Same chrome, sub='Posts by ~<username>'. New server fn wraps list_user_posts_by_tag.
3. Wire both routes into web/src/pages/mod.rs.
4. Integration tests for both server fns.
5. E2E: visit /tags/:tag from a tag chip on a post, confirm only matching posts; use '· here' link to navigate to /~user/tags/:tag, confirm scoping.

title: tags.8: tag display in PostDisplay footer
description:
Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-13-tags-ui-design.md Step 8. Items: 1. Add TagContext enum (SiteWide, ForUser(username)) and thread through PostCard and PostDisplay as a new prop. 2. Add TagList component rendering #:display per tag, plus a '· here' link to /~user/tags/:slug when context is ForUser. 3. Place TagList at the start of
in PostDisplay. 4. Pass TagContext::ForUser(username) from UserTimelinePage, PostPage, draft pages; pass TagContext::SiteWide from HomePage Local mode and from the site-wide tag listing page. 5. CSS: .j-tag (clickable chip in display contexts) and .j-tag-chip (editable chip in TagInput).

title: tags.7: wire TagInput into create/edit forms
description:
Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-13-tags-ui-design.md Step 7.

Items:
1. PostCreateForm (compact branch): place TagInput in the composer bar's tag slot, beneath the textarea, above the action buttons.
2. PostCreateForm (full branch): place TagInput in the right-side aside, between slug and the format toggle.
3. EditPostPage: place TagInput in the matching position, pre-populated with the post's current tags loaded via get_post_preview.
4. E2E (Playwright): create a post with three tags via the UI, submit, refetch the post, assert tags applied.

title: tags.6: TagInput component
description:
Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-13-tags-ui-design.md Step 6.

Items:
1. Add TagInput component. Props: tags: RwSignal<Vec<TagSummary>>, name: &'static str (default 'tags').
2. Render chips with × remove buttons.
3. Text input keydown handling: Enter / Tab create chip; Backspace on empty removes last. Comma is NOT a chip-creation key.
4. Client-side validation against [a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]* after lowercasing (mirrors Tag::from_str); invalid input shows brief inline error.
5. Autocomplete dropdown: 150ms-debounced list_tags(prefix) fetch, keyboard navigation (ArrowUp/Down + Enter), click-to-add.
6. Emit one <input type='hidden' name='tags' value=display> per chip; the form's JSON encoding collects into Vec<String> server-side.
7. Unit tests for pure-function helpers (token splitting, case normalization).

title: tags.5: tags param on create_post + update_post
description:
Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-13-tags-ui-design.md Step 5.

Items:
1. Add tags: Vec<String> param to create_post and update_post.
2. Helper parse_and_validate_tags(tokens) -> Result<Vec<String>, _>: trim whitespace, reject empty tokens, validate each via Tag::from_str, enforce 25-tag cap.
3. create_post: after the post is created, call tag_post for each validated display token.
4. update_post: load existing tags via get_tags_for_post, compute set-diff, call tag_post for newly-added and untag_post for removed.
5. Integration tests: create with tags, update adds + removes, editing only display casing keeps slug stable, exceeds-cap rejection.

title: tags.4: TimelinePostSummary carries tags
description:
Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-13-tags-ui-design.md Step 4.

Items:
1. Add tags: Vec<TagSummary> to TimelinePostSummary.
2. Update list_user_posts, list_local_timeline, list_home_feed, get_post to batch-load tags via PostStorage::get_tags_for_posts (single round-trip per page).
3. Integration tests confirming tags appear on each surface.

title: tags.3: list_tags server fn + TagSummary type
description:
Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-13-tags-ui-design.md Step 3.

Items:
1. Add TagSummary { slug: String, display: String } type (web/src/posts.rs or new web/src/tags.rs).
2. Add #[server(endpoint = '/list_tags', input = Json)] list_tags(prefix: Option<String>, limit: Option<u32>) -> WebResult<Vec<TagSummary>> server fn.
3. Integration tests covering prefix filtering, limit defaults, tags-table-empty case.

title: tags.2: PostStorage list_tags + get_tags_for_posts
description:
Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-13-tags-ui-design.md Step 2.

Items:
1. Add list_tags(prefix: Option<&str>, limit: u32) -> Vec<TagRecord> to PostStorage trait.
2. Add get_tags_for_posts(post_ids: &[i64]) -> HashMap<i64, Vec<PostTag>> to PostStorage trait.
3. Implement on SqlitePostStorage.
4. Implement on PostgresPostStorage.
5. Integration tests on both backends: case-insensitive prefix matching, empty prefix returns alphabetical, limit clamping, batch lookup with mixed post_ids (some with tags, some without, some non-existent).

title: tags.1: JSON-encode create_post + update_post (refactor only)
description:
Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-13-tags-ui-design.md Step 1.

Standalone refactor preceding any tag work. Ships no tag functionality.

Items:
1. Add #[server(input = Json)] to create_post and update_post in web/src/posts.rs.
2. Add post_json(state, path, body, cookie) helper in server/tests/web_posts.rs mirroring post_form but emitting application/json.
3. Rewrite every /api/create_post and /api/update_post call site (~50) to use post_json with serde_json::json!({...}) bodies.
4. scripts/verify passes end-to-end.
5. Commit as a standalone refactor; nothing else lands until this is green.

title: M8.8: End-to-end exercise
description:
See docs/milestones/M8.md Step 8.

Items (M8.8.1 .. M8.8.4):
1. E2E (Playwright): register two users, both publish a post, fetch each per-user feed in all three formats, assert both posts appear at correct URLs in the right order.
2. E2E: with mock hub configured, publish a post, observe a hub ping arrives within ~30s, edit the post, observe a second ping.
3. E2E: 304 short-circuit — fetch a feed, capture ETag, refetch with If-None-Match, assert 304 with empty body.
4. E2E: empty-feed path — fetch a per-user feed for a user with no published posts, assert 200 with a valid empty feed body for each format.

title: M8.7: Feed-event worker (regen + WebSub ping)
description:
See docs/milestones/M8.md Step 7.

Items (M8.7.1 .. M8.7.8):
1. Factor out regenerate_feed(state, feed_url) shared helper that resolves the surface, runs list_published* with the hybrid window, batches tag lookup, serializes via common::feed::render_*, and UPSERTs the feed_cache row. Used by both worker and the handler-miss path from M8.4.
2. Add a job to the existing tokio_cron_scheduler instance (created in M6 for backups). Runs every 10 seconds and processes pending events until queue is empty.
3. For each event, call regenerate_feed(state, event.feed_url) and mark regenerated_at.
4. If feeds.websub_hub_url is set: POST hub.mode=publish&hub.url=<feed URL> (application/x-www-form-urlencoded) with 5s timeout. On 2xx mark pinged_at.
5. On ping failure: increment attempts, set last_error, schedule retry with exponential backoff (1m, 5m, 30m, 2h); after 6 attempts mark with terminal error and stop.
6. If feeds.websub_hub_url is unset: mark pinged_at = regenerated_at so the event is treated as fully complete.
7. Emit observability events on each phase outcome.
8. Integration test using a mock hub (small in-process axum handler) confirming pings arrive with the right body and Content-Type after the cache row is updated.

title: M8.6: Mutation hooks enqueue feed events
description:
See docs/milestones/M8.md Step 6.

Items (M8.6.1 .. M8.6.3):
1. Add helper enqueue_feed_events(state, post, old_tags) that computes the union of affected feed URLs (user feed, site feed, every tag the post has now or had before) x {rss, atom, json} and enqueues one feed_events row per URL.
2. Call from create_post, update_post, publish_post, unpublish_post, delete_post.
3. Integration tests asserting the expected event rows after each mutation, including a tag-edit case where both old-and-new tag feeds are enqueued.

title: M8.5: Feed auto-discovery in HTML pages
description:
See docs/milestones/M8.md Step 5.

Items (M8.5.1 .. M8.5.2):
1. Add <link rel='alternate' type='...' title='...' href='...'> tags to / (site feeds), /~:username/ (user feeds), /tags/:tag (tag feeds), /~:username/tags/:tag (user-tag feeds) — three per page, one per format.
2. E2E test scrapes each page's <head>, follows each link, asserts 200 with the expected content-type.

title: M8.4: Feed handlers
description:
See docs/milestones/M8.md Step 4.

Items (M8.4.1 .. M8.4.4):
1. Add server::feed module with feed_user, feed_site, feed_tag, feed_user_tag axum handlers.
2. Each handler computes the canonical feed_url from path params + format ext, then:
   - On cache hit + matching If-None-Match / If-Modified-Since: 304.
   - On cache hit: 200 with stored body, ETag, Last-Modified, Cache-Control: public, max-age=300, appropriate Content-Type (application/rss+xml, application/atom+xml, application/feed+json).
   - On cache miss: invoke shared regenerate_feed(state, feed_url) (factored out in Step 7) which runs query + serialize + UPSERT and returns the row; respond as for a hit.
3. Wire routes into server::lib: /~:username/feed.{rss,atom,json}, /feed.{rss,atom,json}, /tags/:tag/feed.{rss,atom,json}, /~:username/tags/:tag/feed.{rss,atom,json}.
4. Integration tests per format and per surface: cache-hit, cache-miss (lazy regen), 304 short-circuit on If-None-Match and If-Modified-Since, correct content-type per format.

title: M8.3: site_config keys for feeds
description:
See docs/milestones/M8.md Step 3.

Items (M8.3.1 .. M8.3.3):
1. Define key constants feeds.min_items, feeds.min_days, feeds.websub_hub_url in common::storage::site_config.
2. Add typed helpers loading each with default fallbacks (20, 30, None).
3. Integration tests verifying defaults and overrides.

title: M8.2: feed_cache and feed_events storage
description:
See docs/milestones/M8.md Step 2.

Items (M8.2.1 .. M8.2.8):
1. Define FeedCacheRow and FeedCacheStorage trait (get, upsert, delete) in common::storage::feed_cache.
2. Define FeedEventRecord and FeedEventStorage trait (enqueue, claim_next_pending, mark_regenerated, mark_pinged, mark_failed) in common::storage::feed_events.
3. Create migrations/sqlite/0014_create_feed_cache.sql and migrations/postgres/0014_create_feed_cache.sql.
4. Create migrations/sqlite/0015_create_feed_events.sql and migrations/postgres/0015_create_feed_events.sql.
5. Implement both storage traits on the SQLite backend.
6. Implement both storage traits on the PostgreSQL backend.
7. Add feed_cache: Arc<dyn FeedCacheStorage> and feed_events: Arc<dyn FeedEventStorage> to AppState.
8. Integration tests on both backends: cache upsert/get, event lifecycle (enqueue -> claim -> mark_regenerated -> mark_pinged), retry scheduling on failure.

title: M8.1: Pure feed serializers in common::feed
description:
See docs/milestones/M8.md Step 1.

Items (M8.1.1 .. M8.1.8):
1. Add 'rss' and 'atom_syndication' crates as deps of common.
2. Define FeedMetadata struct (title, description, canonical_url, self_url, hub_url: Option<String>, updated_at).
3. Define FeedItem struct (title, permalink, summary: Option<String>, content_html, published_at, updated_at, tags: Vec<String>).
4. Implement render_rss(metadata, items) -> String.
5. Implement render_atom(metadata, items) -> String.
6. Implement render_json(metadata, items) -> String (JSON Feed 1.1).
7. Add feed_etag(items) helper computing strong validator from (max(updated_at), item count, last post_id).
8. Unit tests: posts with/without titles, with/without tags, multiple items, hub-set vs hub-absent metadata, empty item list.

title: web: add a theme selector to the UI
description:
The codebase ships three CSS themes (terminal, studio, reader — see server/assets/jaunder-themes.css) and the .j-root element is wired to a theme RwSignal with persistence to localStorage (web/src/pages/mod.rs ~line 85-110). The user has no way to choose a theme: DEFAULT_THEME is the only path that ever sets a value, and there is no settings UI, sidebar control, or topbar menu that exposes it.

Proposal: add a small theme picker — probably a j-seg segmented control in /profile or a topbar dropdown in AppShell — that lets the user choose terminal / studio / reader. The wiring already persists the chosen theme via the existing localStorage hook; the picker just needs to call theme.set(...).

Related: jaunder-ka4 — on the deployed binary the data-theme attribute apparently isn't reaching the DOM, so right now even the DEFAULT_THEME is silently absent. Fixing jaunder-ka4 makes the picker meaningful; shipping the picker without jaunder-ka4 would let users select a value that the running server fails to apply.

title: bug(ui): .j-root data-theme attribute is missing on deployed binary
description:
On the nixos-deployed binary, the .j-root element appears to render WITHOUT a data-theme attribute (or with an empty one). Symptoms:
- DevTools shows no --err custom property on the .j-root computed-styles list
- --on-accent still resolves (because :root in jaunder.css supplies it as a fallback) while theme-only tokens silently disappear
- Surfaced visually as jaunder-bxo (filled-red Delete buttons rendering invisibly until --err was backfilled into :root)

Expected: <div class="j-root" data-theme="studio"> on first paint so .j-root[data-theme="studio"] in jaunder-themes.css applies and the full theme variable pack cascades.

Current wiring (web/src/pages/mod.rs):
  let theme = RwSignal::new(DEFAULT_THEME.to_string());
  view! { <div class="j-root" attr:data-theme=move || theme.get()> ... }

Hypotheses to check:
1. attr:<name> reactive bindings may not render the attribute during SSR if the closure produces an empty string at server time (e.g., signal default is dropped between SSR and hydration).
2. The localStorage restore path may set theme to an empty string under some condition before provide_context runs.
3. The deployed binary build path may strip the attr binding (production-only compile flag?).

Fix direction: render data-theme as a static attribute when the value is non-empty (split SSR from client-side reactive update), and/or assert during render that DEFAULT_THEME is non-empty. As a defense-in-depth measure, the :root fallback pack should mirror every token any theme defines, so missing data-theme degrades gracefully instead of breaking specific rules silently.

title: web: unify row-action button styling across list/card pages
description:
Row-action buttons disagree across pages:
- DraftsPage row: Edit/Publish/Delete all 'j-btn is-ghost'
- PostCard (PostPage): Edit/Unpublish/Delete all plain 'j-btn'
- DraftPreviewPage: Publish 'j-btn is-primary', Delete 'j-btn is-ghost'
- MediaPage row (single Delete): 'j-btn is-ghost'
- SessionsPage row (single Revoke): 'j-btn is-ghost'

Style guide §5 says 'is-ghost — Destructive or table-row actions (Delete, Publish)'. DraftsPage / MediaPage / SessionsPage follow the guide; PostCard and DraftPreviewPage drift from it.

Pick a canonical pattern and propagate, then update docs/web-style-guide.md §5 accordingly. Two viable options:
1. Keep current guide (all row actions ghost). Fix PostCard and DraftPreviewPage to match Drafts/Media/Sessions.
2. Differentiate: neutral row actions (Edit, Publish) get plain j-btn; only destructive (Delete, Unpublish) get is-ghost (or introduce is-danger). Fix Drafts/Media/Sessions to match this richer convention. Visually communicates intent.

Note: this issue is closely related to jaunder-h1u (consolidate DraftPreviewPage + PostPage) and jaunder-urw (Unpublish-on-draft); resolving the row-button style first lets those two land cleanly.
notes: Concrete instance on the Media page (/media): the MediaUploadButton renders 'class="j-btn"' (plain) while per-row Delete buttons render 'class="j-btn is-ghost"'. The two button styles sitting next to each other on a single page makes the inconsistency obvious. Any chosen unification needs to resolve this within-page divergence too.

title: bug(ui): PostCard shows 'Unpublish' on draft permalinks; should be 'Publish'
description:
PostCard in web/src/pages/ui.rs renders an Unpublish button whenever is_author is true. PostPage uses PostCard for both published and draft posts, so visiting a draft's permalink as the author surfaces 'Unpublish' on something that is already unpublished — clicking it triggers UnpublishPost which is a no-op or error.

Fix: PostCard should adapt its action column based on draft state. When the post is a draft (post.is_draft or equivalent passed in), render 'Publish' (dispatching PublishPost) instead of 'Unpublish'. Edit and Delete remain unchanged.

Note: post.is_draft isn't currently on TimelinePostSummary; either thread it through (preferred) or derive from published_at being empty. PostPage already computes the draft banner from fetched.is_draft, so the data is available — pass it explicitly to PostCard.

This bug largely disappears if jaunder-h1u (consolidate the two pages) is done by giving PostCard the draft-aware action column it should have had, so wire the action-column fix as part of that consolidation rather than patching twice.

title: web: consolidate DraftPreviewPage and PostPage into a single view
description:
Two pages render essentially the same post:

- /draft/:post_id/preview (DraftPreviewPage) — uses PostDisplay + a custom action column with [Publish, Delete]. Drafts only.
- /~user/YYYY/MM/DD/slug (PostPage) — uses PostCard with [Edit, Unpublish, Delete]. Handles both published and draft posts.

The Drafts listing in DraftsPage links to BOTH a 'Preview' (DraftPreviewPage) and a 'Permalink' (PostPage) for the same draft, surfacing arbitrary differences in chrome and actions. Both already share PostDisplay underneath; the only real divergence is the action column.

Proposal: drop DraftPreviewPage and route /draft/:post_id/preview to PostPage (after resolving post_id → permalink server-side, or by letting PostCard accept a post_id-keyed load path). Update the Drafts listing to link to the canonical permalink only. Folds the fix for the 'Unpublish on a draft' bug (see follow-up issue) into PostCard's action column so it adapts to draft state.

title: web: compose toolbar layout — group inline composer's action buttons
description:
The compact PostCreateForm rendered by InlineComposer (authenticated home page) lays out its children as a single flex row inside `.j-composer-body`:

  [textarea] [j-seg Markdown|Org] [j-composer-toolbar Save-draft + Publish stacked] [MediaPanel attach]

The format toggle is horizontal, the action buttons are stacked, and the media-attach button sits in a third column — three different control geometries side-by-side. The result reads as scattered controls.

Redesign so the textarea spans the full body width (no flex siblings to its right) and a single horizontal toolbar row underneath the textarea contains, left-to-right: format toggle, attach-media button, spacer, Save draft, Publish (primary). Update .j-composer-body / .j-composer-toolbar accordingly in jaunder.css and rework the compact branch of PostCreateForm in web/src/pages/ui.rs.

title: web: style the /register form with a proper card layout
description: RegisterPage in web/src/pages/auth.rs has the same bare-label problem as LoginPage. The invite-only branch adds a conditional "Invite code" field, so the layout has to handle a variable field set.\n\nApply the same card idiom as the /login fix: j-card + j-card-head + body with field rows + footer with the primary submit. Share the label/input/field-row classes with /login (introduce generic .j-form-field / .j-form-input rules in jaunder.css if needed, rather than re-purposing the .j-backup-* names).

title: web: style the /login form with a proper card layout
description: LoginPage in web/src/pages/auth.rs uses bare `<label>` + `<input>` elements with no design-system classes, so the form gets default browser styling and sits flush against the page gutter.\n\nApply the settings-card idiom (see backup.rs / docs/web-style-guide.md §4): wrap the ActionForm in `<div class="j-card">` with a `j-card-head` header containing the form title, a body with properly styled label/input pairs, and a footer row that right-aligns the primary submit button.

title: web: add j-btn class to plain submit buttons across forms
description: Several pages have unstyled `<button type="submit">"…"</button>` instead of `<button type="submit" class="j-btn is-primary">"…"</button>`. Per docs/web-style-guide.md §5, every form's primary submit should wear `j-btn is-primary`.\n\nKnown call sites: auth.rs (Register, Login), email.rs (EmailPage), invites.rs (CreateInvite), password_reset.rs (Forgot/Reset), profile.rs (UpdateProfile), sessions.rs (RevokeSession row buttons — these should likely be `is-ghost` as row actions, not `is-primary`).

title:
web: replace
blocks with
description:
Two flash-message shapes coexist: - `

"…"

` (used widely) - `
…
` (used in posts.rs:53 with inline padding, and posts.rs:595 Edit page draft-saved block) The div-form wraps multiple paragraphs and a link. Refactor those compound success blocks into a layout that doesn't piggyback on a flash class — either a dedicated success-summary component or a plain styled section — and reserve `

` for single-sentence confirmations per docs/web-style-guide.md §3.

title: web: unify loading placeholders to j-loading + U+2026
description:
`"Loading..."` (three ASCII dots, no class) is used in auth.rs, email.rs, invites.rs, profile.rs, sessions.rs, and several spots in posts.rs (PostPage, UserTimelinePage, DraftPreviewPage, Load-more button text). Standardize on `

"Loading\u{2026}"

` per docs/web-style-guide.md §2.

title: web: migrate legacy pages to Topbar + j-scroll chrome
description: Several pages still use bare `<h1>` headers and no body wrapper instead of the standard `Topbar` + `j-scroll` chrome. See docs/web-style-guide.md §1. Pages to migrate: auth (Register/Login/Logout), email (EmailPage/VerifyEmailPage), profile, sessions, invites, password_reset (Forgot/Reset), and the three in posts.rs that still use the legacy style — PostPage, UserTimelinePage, DraftPreviewPage.

title: docs: explain primary-vs-secondary button choice on unauth landing
description: On the unauthenticated home page (web/src/pages/home.rs, Local mode), the Topbar right-side actions are: "Sign in" "Register" The styling difference is intentional and follows docs/web-style-guide.md §5 ("one primary action per surface"): the unauth landing is biased toward signup, so Register is the primary CTA and Sign in is the secondary affordance for returning users. Reviewer flagged this as visually inconsistent, which suggests the rule isn't sufficiently discoverable. Task: extend §5 of docs/web-style-guide.md (or add a short "button hierarchy on landing pages" subsection) so this choice reads as intentional next time. No code change unless we decide to revisit the convention.

title: web: support reactive Topbar title (for UserTimelinePage)
description:
UserTimelinePage uses an inline `<div class="j-topbar">` with a reactive `<h1>` because Topbar's `title` prop is typed `String`, not `Signal<String>` / `MaybeSignal<String>`, so it can't accept a closure that depends on URL params.

To bring UserTimelinePage back under the Topbar component, generalize Topbar:
- change `title: String` and `sub: Option<String>` to a signal-aware type (`MaybeSignal<String>` with `#[prop(into)]`, or similar)
- ensure existing static-string call sites still compile
- swap the inline j-topbar block at web/src/pages/posts.rs UserTimelinePage back to <Topbar>

title: web: replace inline-styled media table with a reusable .j-table class
description: `MediaPage` in web/src/pages/media.rs renders an ad-hoc table where every `<tr>/<th>/<td>` carries an inline style attribute (`border-collapse`, `padding`, `border-bottom`). Drafts also has a row-based layout but uses `.j-draft-row` classes.\n\nFold the inline styling into a `.j-table` (or extend `.j-card`) so Sessions, Invites, Media, and any future list views share one look.

title: web: extract repeated page-gutter inline styles into a CSS class
description: `style="padding:16px 32px"` is repeated in media.rs:46, posts.rs:53, posts.rs:632 (now drafts), and the inner gutter of any page that follows the Topbar + j-scroll pattern. Per docs/web-style-guide.md §7, repeated layout patterns belong in a class. Suggest adding `.j-page` (or extending `.j-scroll > div`) so the boilerplate disappears.

title: web: stop passing sub=String::new() to Topbar
description:
`EditPostPage` in web/src/pages/posts.rs:457 calls ``. The empty string still flows through Topbar's `sub.map(|s| view! {
{s}
})` and renders an empty `j-sub` div, occupying vertical space.\n\nEither omit `sub` (the prop is `#[prop(optional)] sub: Option`) or give Edit a real subtitle (e.g. "Long-form" to match New post).

value: When scripts/e2e-local.sh or cargo leptos end-to-end exhibits unexplained test failures (especially auth/register failing in ways that don't track to a code change), check for lingering jaunder processes from previous runs (`pgrep -f 'target.*jaunder'`). The local wrapper creates a per-run temp DB, but an old jaunder process bound to port 3000 keeps serving requests against the OLD database, causing register/login/post-creation tests to fail mysteriously while the build itself looks clean. Always `pkill -f 'target.*jaunder' || true` before retrying e2e locally.

Anti-pattern: Effect::new_isomorphic copying Resource value into RwSignals causes SSR tokio-rt-worker panics. Why: the Resource future can resolve on a tokio worker after the per-request reactive owner is disposed, and the isomorphic effect fires post-disposal, calling get_untracked/set on a dead signal and panicking with 'you tried to access a reactive value which was defined at ..., but it has already been disposed' (reactive_graph traits.rs:361). How to spot: any Effect::new_isomorphic(|_| { if let Some(x) = resource.try_get().flatten() { signal.set(...) } }). Originally observed at web/src/pages/home.rs:49 and web/src/pages/posts.rs:228,249,423 — fixed in commits 6ba0c03 and 199ec9c on the 'feeds' branch (2026-05-11).

Preferred pattern for Resource-derived initial state with client-side mutation: use plain Effect::new (NOT Effect::new_isomorphic) to copy a Resource's resolved value into RwSignals. SSR will render whatever placeholder the view produces from the signals' initial values (e.g. a 'Loading...' state). The Resource still fetches during SSR but its value is re-resolved on the client, where Effect::new fires after hydration and seeds the signals. This avoids the disposal race entirely because the effect never runs server-side. Reference: post-fix web/src/pages/home.rs and web/src/pages/posts.rs (commits 6ba0c03, 199ec9c on 'feeds' branch). Keep the original-style conditional-set patterns (e.g. 'if mode != new_mode { mode.set(new_mode) }') to prevent spurious signal updates from re-running outer view closures and remounting child components like InlineComposer.

Local cargo leptos end-to-end uses scripts/e2e-local.sh wrapper (per-run temp storage dir) so the test suite starts from clean state, matching the Nix VM e2e environment under scripts/verify. The wrapper exports JAUNDER_STORAGE_PATH, JAUNDER_DB, JAUNDER_DB_PATH, JAUNDER_MAIL_CAPTURE_FILE to a mktemp dir and cleans up on exit. end2end/run-e2e.sh and the Nix flake's seed_db both delegate fixture creation (testlogin, testnoemail, registration_policy=open, clear mail capture) to scripts/seed-e2e-fixtures.sh — single source of truth. Don't reintroduce hardcoded DELETE FROM table SQL in the seed step; the temp-dir-per-run approach makes that unnecessary.

Anti-pattern (SSR-eager Resource calling expect_context::<Arc<AppState>>): when a Leptos Resource invoking a server fn is rendered eagerly on every page (e.g. BackupBanner, Sidebar), the Resource fetcher runs on a tokio worker via Suspense, and on client disconnect / e2e parallelism the per-request reactive owner can be disposed before the worker reaches the expect_context call — panic 'expected context of type Arc<AppState> to be present'. Fix: replace expect_context::<Arc<AppState>>() with a small helper that returns InternalResult (use_context::<Arc<AppState>>().ok_or_else(|| InternalError::server_message("missing AppState context"))). The error path is already harmless (Sidebar/BackupBanner treat Err as 'not operator / no banner'). Do NOT switch to LocalResource as a structural fix — LocalResource awaited inside an SSR-rendered Suspense never resolves on the server, hanging the entire Suspense subtree and breaking many e2e tests with hydration timeouts. Reference: web/src/backup.rs after jaunder-3gt revisit; jaunder-3gt closed by defensive use_context, not by LocalResource.
